title: Baby Jaundice and Herbal Essential Oils: 11 Remedies to Help

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruta graveolens essential oils, also known as rue essential oil, may help with jaundice due to its properties that support liver health and promote the removal of bilirubin from the body. This oil contains constituents like rutaecarpine and limonene, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These properties can help reduce liver congestion and improve the flow of bile, allowing bilirubin to be eliminated from the body. If you're considering using Ruta graveolens essential oils for jaundice, it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any action. In general, you can add a few drops of the oil to a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the skin in a gentle, circular motion. You can also inhale the oil by adding a few drops to a diffuser or mixing it with water and inhaling the vapor. It's essential to note that the oil can be quite potent, so start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed. When using Ruta graveolens essential oils for jaundice, you may start to notice improvements in your condition, such as reduced yellowing of the skin and eyes, and increased energy levels. The oil may also help reduce liver inflammation and promote the growth of new liver cells. However, it's crucial to be patient and consistent with your treatment, as the effects may take some time to manifest.
remedies.image_alt: Ruta graveolens herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils, also known as lemon balm, contains properties and constituents like linalool, linalyl acetate, and beta-pinene that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms. Jaundice occurs when a buildup of bilirubin in the blood causes yellowing of the skin and eyes. Melissa officinalis essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help reduce inflammation and promote liver health. This may aid in reducing bilirubin levels and alleviating jaundice symptoms. If you or your child is experiencing jaundice, consult with a healthcare professional before using Melissa officinalis essential oils. They may recommend diluting the essential oil with a carrier oil and applying it topically to the skin or using it in a steam inhalation to help reduce inflammation and promote liver function. However, it's essential to note that jaundice can be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, and seeking medical attention is crucial for proper diagnosis and treatment. Using Melissa officinalis essential oils for jaundice may lead to a reduction in inflammation, improved liver function, and alleviation of jaundice symptoms. However, it's essential to follow the guidance of a healthcare professional and monitor the situation closely. If symptoms persist or worsen, seek immediate medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils, commonly known as ginger oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate jaundice in newborn babies. Jaundice occurs when a buildup of bilirubin in the blood causes the skin and eyes to turn yellow. Ginger oil's active properties, such as gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce bilirubin levels and promote liver function. The oil's constituents, including sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, also have a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can be beneficial in cases of jaundice caused by digestive issues. If you suspect that your newborn baby has jaundice, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. If advised to use ginger oil, apply a few drops to your baby's skin, preferably on the soles of the feet or on the abdomen. Gently massage the oil into the skin to promote absorption. You can also add a few drops of ginger oil to your baby's bathwater or mix it with a carrier oil for a gentle massage. Using ginger oil may lead to a reduction in bilirubin levels and a subsequent improvement in your baby's jaundice symptoms. You may notice a decrease in yellowing of the skin and eyes, and your baby's energy levels may increase. However, it's crucial to note that ginger oil should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. Always consult your healthcare professional before using ginger oil or any other herbal remedy, especially for newborn babies.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils, also known as milk thistle oil, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate jaundice symptoms. This oil contains compounds like flavonolignans and silymarin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin is especially beneficial as it helps protect liver cells from damage and promotes liver function. When used topically or taken internally in moderation, Silybum marianum essential oils may aid in reducing jaundice symptoms by promoting liver detoxification and improving bile flow. If you're considering using Silybum marianum essential oils for jaundice, here's what to do: dilute the oil with a carrier oil, apply it to the skin in gentle circular motions, or take it internally in a small amount (consult a healthcare professional for the right dosage). It's essential to note that these oils should not replace medical treatment but be used in conjunction with it.  When using Silybum marianum essential oils, you may experience improved liver function, reduced jaundice symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being. However, it's crucial to monitor your condition closely and adjust the dosage as needed. In some cases, you may also experience side effects like stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions. If you experience any severe symptoms, discontinue use and consult a healthcare professional immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus essential oils, also known as artichoke oil, has been studied for its potential benefits in treating jaundice in newborns. Jaundice occurs when a buildup of bilirubin in the blood causes yellowing of the skin and eyes. Cynara scolymus essential oils contain properties like sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which may help reduce bilirubin levels and improve liver function. The sesquiterpenes, specifically cynarin and luteolin, have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning they help protect the liver from damage. Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol may also contribute to the oil's antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress on the liver. If you're considering using Cynara scolymus essential oils for jaundice, consult with a healthcare professional or a licensed aromatherapist to determine the best course of action. They may recommend a diluted solution of the oil, typically 1-2% concentration, applied topically to the skin or used in a carrier oil for massage. However, it's essential to note that Cynara scolymus essential oils should not be ingested, as it may cause adverse effects. Also, consult with a healthcare professional before using the oil on newborns, as their skin is sensitive and may react to the oil. Taking action with Cynara scolymus essential oils may lead to a decrease in bilirubin levels, improved liver function, and reduced jaundice symptoms. However, it's crucial to monitor the newborn's condition closely and adjust treatment plans as necessary. Regular check-ups with a healthcare professional will help determine the effectiveness of the treatment and make any necessary adjustments.
remedies.image_alt: Cynara scolymus herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils have been traditionally used to help alleviate jaundice in infants. The oils contain properties and constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have a positive effect on the liver's ability to process bilirubin, a pigment responsible for the yellow discoloration associated with jaundice. When used in moderation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to the development of jaundice.  To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for jaundice, it's recommended to dilute a few drops of the oil in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and gently massage it onto the baby's skin in a circular motion. This can be done 2-3 times a day, for a few minutes at a time. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils on an infant, as they can be potent and may interact with other medications. When using Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for jaundice, parents can expect to see a reduction in the yellow discoloration of the baby's skin and eyes, and an improvement in their overall mood and appetite. In some cases, the use of Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help to accelerate the resolution of jaundice, allowing the baby to be discharged from the hospital sooner. However, it's crucial to monitor the baby's condition closely and adjust the treatment plan as needed. If the jaundice persists or worsens, parents should seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium essential oils have been studied for their potential in treating jaundice due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil is rich in constituents such as thujone, borneol, and limonene, which are believed to help reduce liver inflammation and promote the removal of bilirubin from the body. The antiseptic properties of the oil may also help prevent bacterial overgrowth in the gut, which can contribute to jaundice in some cases. If you're considering using Artemisia absinthium essential oils for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional first, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding. To use the oil safely, dilute it with a carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) and apply it topically to the liver area or take it internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional. The recommended dosage is typically 5-10 drops of the diluted oil, taken 2-3 times a day.  It's essential to monitor your liver function and bilirubin levels regularly while using the oil. Some people may experience relief from jaundice symptoms, such as dark urine and pale stools, within a few days to a week. However, results may vary, and in some cases, jaundice may persist or worsen.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils, also known as licorice root oil, may help alleviate jaundice symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains constituents like glycyrrhizin, licochalcone A, and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow. This can help break down bilirubin, a pigment responsible for jaundice. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils for jaundice, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it onto the skin over the liver area. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially for infants or children, as they may be more sensitive to the oil's effects. If you decide to try Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils, monitor your child's symptoms closely and seek medical attention if they worsen or don't improve within a few days. You may notice a decrease in jaundice symptoms, such as a decrease in yellowing of the skin and eyes, and an improvement in appetite and overall well-being. However, it's crucial to note that essential oils should not replace medical treatment for jaundice, and you should continue to follow your healthcare provider's advice.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamaemelum nobile essential oils, also known as Roman chamomile, contain properties that may help alleviate jaundice in newborns. The essential oil is rich in chamazulene and bisabolol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. These properties may help reduce inflammation in the liver and digestive tract, which can contribute to jaundice. Additionally, chamazulene has been shown to have a mild diuretic effect, which can help to increase urine production and remove bilirubin from the body more efficiently. If you suspect that your newborn has jaundice, consult with a healthcare professional before using Chamaemelum nobile essential oils. To use the oil, you can add a few drops to a warm bath or mix it with a carrier oil for a gentle massage. However, it's essential to note that the oil should not be used undiluted on the skin, and should not be ingested.  When using Chamaemelum nobile essential oils for jaundice, you can expect to see a reduction in the severity of symptoms, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and an improvement in overall liver function. However, if jaundice persists or worsens, seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Chamaemelum nobile herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata essential oils have been traditionally used to help alleviate jaundice in infants. This is due to its high concentration of saponins, flavonoids, and glycosides, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The main constituents of Cassia auriculata essential oils include auriculin A and B, cassiadin, and cassiposide, which have been shown to stimulate liver function and promote the breakdown of bilirubin, the pigment responsible for jaundice. If you suspect jaundice in an infant, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Cassia auriculata essential oils. However, if advised to use it, dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil and apply it topically to the infant's abdomen in a gentle, circular motion.  Monitor the infant's symptoms closely, and seek immediate medical attention if they show signs of worsening jaundice, such as increased vomiting, lethargy, or changes in skin color. If used correctly, Cassia auriculata essential oils may help to reduce jaundice symptoms within a few days, allowing the infant to recover and return to their normal activities. However, if jaundice persists or worsens, further medical evaluation and treatment may be necessary to rule out underlying conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Cassia auriculata herbal essential oils for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, also known as lemon grass oil, may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice in newborns. Jaundice occurs when bilirubin levels in the blood become too high, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contain properties like limonene, beta-pinene, and citral, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These constituents may help reduce liver enzymes and bilirubin levels, promoting the liver's ability to remove waste products from the blood.  To use Cymbopogon citratus essential oils for jaundice, consult a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist, as they can recommend a safe dosage and application method. In general, a few drops of the oil can be added to a warm bath or used in a gentle massage.  When using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils for jaundice, monitor your baby's condition closely, as it may take some time to notice improvements. Keep in mind that jaundice can be a sign of an underlying issue, so it's essential to continue with medical treatment as advised by a healthcare professional. If you notice any changes or worsening symptoms, seek immediate medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for jaundice

intro_desc: Babies can get jaundice, a condition where their skin turns yellow due to too much bilirubin in their blood. This happens when their liver isn't working properly, and it can't remove the bilirubin. As a result, the bilirubin builds up and causes the yellow color. Jaundice is common in newborns, and it usually goes away on its own within a few weeks. However, in some cases, it can lead to serious health problems if left untreated. Babies with jaundice may seem okay, but they can be feeling unwell and uncomfortable. They may have trouble feeding, be irritable, and even have trouble sleeping. In severe cases, jaundice can cause brain damage or other complications, so it's essential to get it checked out.  When a baby has jaundice, their parents and healthcare providers may try various treatments. These can include light therapy, which exposes the baby to a special light that helps break down the bilirubin, and fluids to help the baby pass the bilirubin through their urine. However, some parents are looking for more natural ways to help their baby feel better. That's where herbal essential oils come in. These oils are derived from plants and can be used to help support the baby's liver function and reduce the bilirubin levels. They can also help to soothe and calm the baby, making them feel more comfortable and relaxed. When used properly and under the guidance of a healthcare provider, these oils may be a helpful addition to the baby's treatment plan.
